ev.headline: Sodium-ion EV battery breakthrough delivers 11-min charging and 450 km range
ev.summary: CATL and Chinese automakers advance sodium-ion battery technology for mass production with faster charging speeds and competitive range capabilities.

ev.headline: Solid-state EV battery that can achieve 800 miles of driving range becomes a reality
ev.summary: Changan Automobile announced solid-state battery with 932-mile CLTC range and plans trial installations by Q3 2026.

ev.headline: All The EVs That Got Canceled Or Delayed In 2025 And 2026
ev.summary: Major automakers continue to cancel or delay EV models as consumer demand slows and policy uncertainty increases.

ev.headline: Honda And Sony Are Killing Their Two Afeela Electric Cars
ev.summary: Sony Honda Mobility discontinues Afeela EV development following Honda's reassessment of its electric vehicle strategy.

ev.headline: Electric car rollout risks failing disabled drivers as charging chaos causes 'real-world constraints'
ev.summary: UK councils report struggles to offer accessible EV charging spaces for disabled drivers amid rollout acceleration.

ev.headline: Hazmat responds to electric vehicle fire at Audi dealership
ev.summary: An electric vehicle fire at an Audi dealership prompted shelter-in-place orders while HAZMAT assessed lithium-ion battery contamination risks.

ev.headline: Bipartisan legislation expanding direct EV sales in Washington signed into law
ev.summary: Washington passes law allowing Rivian and Lucid to sell vehicles directly to consumers, estimated to increase EV adoption by 13% by 2030.

ev.headline: Out of Charge: Key Issues to Successfully Monetize Public EV Charging in 2026
ev.summary: Analysis shows EV sales reached 1.3 million in 2025 but charging infrastructure monetization remains critical challenge with 47% of consumers citing range anxiety.

ev.headline: Big 3 automakers take $52.1 billion hit from EV pivot
ev.summary: GM, Ford, and Stellantis collectively absorb $52.1 billion in charges after reversing aggressive EV strategies due to weak demand and policy changes.

ev.headline: Detroit's $114 billion problem: The EV strategy that backfired
ev.summary: Seven major automakers lost $114 billion on EV investments between 2022-2025, averaging $20,887 loss per vehicle sold.

ev.headline: GM takes $7.2 billion charge on EV strategy shift and restructuring
ev.summary: General Motors announces massive charge related to EV capacity realignment due to declining consumer demand and Trump administration policy changes.

ev.headline: What's next for EV batteries in 2026
ev.summary: New battery chemistries including sodium-ion and solid-state batteries gain traction as US EV market faces headwinds following federal tax credit sunset.

ev.headline: Top 10 EV Battery Trends in 2025 and What We Can Expect in 2026
ev.summary: LFP battery deployment surpassed nickel-based chemistries for first time with prices dropping 8% to $108/kWh.

ev.headline: Chinese EV maker plans 620-mile solid-state battery cars in 2026
ev.summary: Dongfeng plans production EV with solid-state battery delivering 621-mile range and ultra-high voltage charging platform.

ev.headline: What's Next in 2026: Battery and EV Tech
ev.summary: CATL announces sodium-ion battery mass production while solid-state batteries progress closer to commercial availability.

ev.headline: CES 2026 Round-up: new EV charger and new solid-state battery
ev.summary: Autel Energy launches new AC/DC charging solutions while ProLogium partners on solid-state batteries for e-bikes and light EVs.

ev.headline: US Auto Production Trends: EV Strategy and Changes
ev.summary: US automakers reset EV strategies as policy changes boost ICE and hybrid production while slowing EV adoption rates.

ev.headline: Electric vehicle and battery supply chain: 5 things to look for in 2026
ev.summary: 2026 marks technological turning point with sodium-ion battery scale-up and first semi-solid-state battery mass-market deliveries.

ev.headline: 5 auto industry trends to watch in 2026
ev.summary: Automakers shift to hybrid models as EV sales cool; hybrid vehicles expected to account for 34% of US passenger vehicles by 2034.

ev.headline: Trump administration moves to require 100% domestic materials in EV chargers
ev.summary: Federal Highway Administration proposes raising domestic content requirement for EV chargers from 55% to 100% for federal funding eligibility.

ev.headline: President Trump & Transportation Secretary unveil 'Freedom Means Affordable Cars' Initiative
ev.summary: Trump administration proposes new CAFE standards reset projected to save $109 billion over five years and reduce new vehicle costs by $1,000 average.

ev.headline: Good News, Drivers: It's the End of the Road for the EV Mandate
ev.summary: Trump EPA takes steps toward terminating Biden's EV mandate targeting 56% electric vehicle sales by 2032.

v2x.headline: California Energy Commission Releases Roadmap to Unlocking the Benefits of Bidirectional Charging
v2x.summary: The CEC released a roadmap on March 5, 2026, assessing bidirectional EV charging opportunities and identifying barriers. GM announced a Chevy Bolt with bidirectional capability priced below $30,000 for 2026, and nearly its entire EV lineup is now bidirectional-capable.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen Bets On Grid-Integrated Battery Storage
v2x.summary: Volkswagen is pivoting from EV production focus toward broader roles in battery energy storage and grid integration through a new facility in Salzgitter, Germany. The company demonstrates how industrial capacity can serve households and the electricity grid simultaneously.

v2x.headline: Used Electric Vehicle Batteries Bolstering Texas Power Grid
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions deployed 24 megawatt-hours of second-life EV batteries in Texas for grid storage at competitive costs. The company plans to expand to 100 megawatt-hours of storage capacity across Texas to power approximately 3,300 homes per day.

v2x.headline: U.S. Battery Industry Cuts Losses, Shifts to New Ventures Amid EV Bust
v2x.summary: While EV sales disappointed, battery energy storage systems demand surged in 2025 with strong growth expected in 2026. Automakers and battery companies are shifting production toward lithium-iron-phosphate batteries for grid storage and data center applications.

v2x.headline: GM Energy Open to Leasing Residential Batteries and Bidirectional EV Chargers
v2x.summary: General Motors announced leasing programs for GM Energy home energy management systems and PowerBank residential batteries. The initiative removes high upfront costs and makes bidirectional technology accessible to more homeowners beyond just GM EV owners.

v2x.headline: Enphase Slates Volume Production of Bidirectional EV Chargers for Q4 2026
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy plans to start volume manufacturing of its IQ Bidirectional EV Charging Platform in Q4 2026 after successful demonstrations. The DC-based charger supports home backup and future grid services with global interoperability standards.

v2x.headline: Nissan to Launch Affordable Vehicle-to-Grid Technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan became the first automotive company to gain G99 Grid code certification with an AC-based V2G solution. The company aims to roll out affordable AC bidirectional chargers at prices comparable to traditional mono-directional chargers.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging 2026: Which Cars Can Power Your Home and the Grid?
v2x.summary: In 2026, bidirectional charging support remains limited with full V2H/V2G capability available only in select brands including BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Ford, Nissan, and Hyundai/Kia. Availability depends on hardware, firmware, and regional programs.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Home Is the Bidirectional Upgrade You'll Use in 2026
v2x.summary: V2H is becoming the primary bidirectional upgrade for households in 2026 with fewer barriers than V2G. It provides backup power and energy bill savings without requiring grid export permission or virtual power plant participation.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz Launches Bidirectional Charging for Home Use
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz launched MB.CHARGE, a digital charging ecosystem featuring bidirectional functionality. The new all-electric GLC will be the first model, with the market launch beginning in Germany, France, and the United Kingdom.

v2x.headline: Which Electric Cars Can Charge Bidirectionally?
v2x.summary: A comprehensive guide showing that by October 2025, multiple automakers have announced bidirectional capabilities. Mercedes-Benz, BMW, BYD, and others are rolling out V2G solutions with infrastructure in major cities like Utrecht in the Netherlands.

v2x.headline: V2G Technology Raises $5.8 Billion Market Potential by 2036
v2x.summary: The global bidirectional EV charging market is projected to reach $5.8 billion by 2036, with 2026 identified as the critical takeoff point for mass smart grid integration. Every parked EV could become a distributed energy resource stabilizing infrastructure.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Market Set to Reach US$65.84 Billion by 2035
v2x.summary: The global V2G market was valued at $6.27 billion in 2025 and is expected to reach $65.84 billion by 2035, growing at 26.50% annually. Europe mandated ISO 15118-20 for public chargers in summer 2025, forcing universal interoperability.

v2x.headline: Battery Storage Projects Surge as Utilities Prepare for Next Grid Era
v2x.summary: Battery energy storage deployment accelerated in 2025 with more than 10 gigawatts installed, exceeding 2024 totals by mid-2025. Large-scale projects are underway across the U.S. from California to Florida for grid stability and resilience.

v2x.headline: V2G in 2025: The Missing Piece that Finally Connects Electromobility and Smart Grids
v2x.summary: 2025 marked the year of tangible V2G implementation with pilots transitioning to full-scale operations. Europe dominates as the largest V2G laboratory with 500 bidirectional Renaults operating commercially in Utrecht's car-sharing scheme.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicles as Grid Resources: Barriers to V2G in the United States
v2x.summary: A comprehensive study found V2G adoption limited to small pilots in the U.S. due to socio-technical and institutional barriers. The U.S. lacks market frameworks that properly value and compensate energy services EVs can provide despite two decades of technical demonstrations.

v2x.headline: Redwood Materials Expands Battery Recycling for Energy Storage
v2x.summary: Redwood Materials expanded its battery recycling and reuse program in March 2026 to supply second-life EV batteries for large-scale energy storage systems supporting data centers. The initiative focuses on recovering lithium, nickel, and cobalt for circular supply chains.

v2x.headline: Driving Energy Forward: An Introduction to Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G)
v2x.summary: V2G technology enables EV owners to reduce energy costs, provide backup power during outages, and stabilize the grid during peak demand. Multiple utilities including National Grid, PG&E, and BGE are running pilot programs demonstrating financial and grid benefits.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for Home Backup and Grid Services
v2x.summary: Enphase introduced its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger architecture supporting V2H and V2G capabilities. The DC charger connects directly to vehicle ports and manages charging, home backup, and grid services through the Enphase App, launching in H2 2026.

v2x.headline: Massachusetts Launches First-of-Its-Kind Statewide V2X Pilot Program
v2x.summary: Massachusetts launched a two-year V2X pilot program backed by the Massachusetts Clean Energy Center to deploy 100 bidirectional chargers to homes, schools, and towns. The initiative aims to turn EVs into grid assets with free charger installations.

v2x.headline: Toyota's Vehicle-to-Grid Pilot Demonstrates Grid Stability Benefits
v2x.summary: Toyota introduced its next phase of V2G pilots with Texas-based Oncor, demonstrating how BEVs provide grid stability like virtual power plants. The company is testing at multiple utilities including San Diego Gas & Electric and Pepco in Maryland.
